records.ety: [L. infusus, p. p. of infundere to pour in or into; pref. in- in + fundere to pour: cf. F. infuser. See Found to cast.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Infused (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Infusing.]

defs: To pour in, as a liquid; to pour (into or upon); to shed.
quotes: That strong Circean liquor cease to infuse.

defs: To instill, as principles or qualities; to introduce.
quotes: That souls of animals infuse themselves Into the trunks of men.
Why should he desire to have qualities infused into his son which himself never possessed?

defs: To inspire; to inspirit or animate; to fill; -- followed by with.
quotes: Infusing him with self and vain conceit.

defs: To steep in water or other fluid without boiling, for the propose of extracting medicinal qualities; to soak.
quotes: One scruple of dried leaves is infused in ten ounces of warm water.

defs: To make an infusion with, as an ingredient; to tincture; to saturate.
